text.content:
Character Backgrounds

The backgrounds in the Player's Handbook work fine for characters in this adventure, but the following notes describe how each background fits into the Icewind Dale setting. Each background also suggests a character hook — a reason why that character has come to or is living in Icewind Dale.

BackgroundCharacter Hook
AcolyteAuril the Frostmaiden has blessed me, or so I believe. I came to Icewind Dale to be closer to her.
CharlatanI'm running a cons in Ten-Towns, selling fake remedies for frostbite and whale oil lanterns that don't work in the cold.
CriminalI fled to Icewind Dale to escape the law. No one follows you this far north.
EntertainerI came north to perform for the isolated folk of Ten-Towns, who are starved for entertainment.
Folk HeroI saved my hometown from a terrible monster or disaster. Now I seek new challenges.
Guild ArtisanI came to Ten-Towns to sell my wares. The markets are sparse but the prices are high.
HermitI retreated to Icewind Dale to find solitude and contemplate the mysteries of the universe.
NobleI was exiled from polite society and ended up here. It's not what I'm used to.
OutlanderI grew up among the Reghed nomads or in the mountains with the goliaths.
SageI came to study the Everlasting Rime and Auril's magic. It's unprecedented.
SailorI sailed the Sea of Moving Ice and got stranded when my ship was destroyed by an iceberg.
SoldierI was stationed in Icewind Dale as part of a garrison and never left.
UrchinI was born and raised in Ten-Towns. I've never known anything but winter.

Goliath Player Characters

Goliaths from the Spine of the World are a common sight in Icewind Dale. Goliath player characters in this adventure come from one of two rival settlements: Skytower Shelter or Wyrmdoom Crag, both described in Chapter 2. A goliath from one settlement is familiar with its inhabitants and their longstanding feud with the rival settlement.

Goliaths speak both Common and Giant. They are typically neutral good or lawful neutral, and they place a high value on personal achievement and the good of the community.

Character Secrets

Appendix B contains secrets that you can photocopy, cut out, and hand to your players at the start of the adventure. Each secret provides a character with a special trait, ability, or piece of information that ties them to the world of Icewind Dale. A character can have only one secret.

The seventeen secrets are:

  • Alagondar Scion — Descended from the last lord of Neverwinter.
  • Doppelganger — You are secretly a doppelganger who has forgotten their true nature.
  • Drizzt Fan — Obsessed with Drizzt Do'Urden, you've come north to walk in his footsteps.
  • Elusive Paramour — Someone very dangerous is looking for you.
  • Escaped Prisoner — You escaped from Revel's End, the prison on the Arctic coast.
  • Littlest Yeti — You were raised by yetis. You speak Yeti and have advantage on Charisma checks to influence yetis.
  • Midwinter Child — Born on Midwinter, you have resistance to cold damage.
  • Old Flame — You have a lover in Ten-Towns whom you haven't seen in years.
  • Orc Stone — You carry a magic stone that can summon an orc war chief spirit.
  • Owlbear Whisperer — Owlbears are predisposed to like you.
  • Pirate Cannibal — You survived a shipwreck by eating your crewmates. The ship's treasure is still out there.
  • Reghed Heir — You are the offspring of Queen Bjornhild Solvigsdottir of the Tiger Tribe.
  • Reincarnation — You died and were brought back by a druid's reincarnate spell.
  • Ring Hunter — You seek a knucklehead trout that swallowed your father's signet ring.
  • Runaway Author — You wrote a scandalous exposé and now have powerful enemies.
  • Slaad Host — A red slaad implanted an egg in you. You have two months.
  • Spy — You are a spy for an outside organization operating in Icewind Dale.

Images for all 17 secret cards are available in the module's asset library and can be handed out as player handouts.
